# Fraud Alert Raised Event

**Definition.** Fraud Alert Raised Event is the business event signalling that a Fraud Alert has been generated by detection scoring and routed to investigation, triggering case adjudication.

**Also known as:** Alert Raised Event, Fraud Flag Event.

## Relationships
- Fraud Alert Raised Event is related to the Risk Management domain.
- Fraud Alert Raised Event causes Adjudicate Fraud Alert.
- Fraud Alert Raised Event mentions Fraud Alert.

## Details
Fraud Alert Raised Event is emitted at the hand-off between detection and investigation, once real-time scoring has breached thresholds, a Fraud Alert has been generated and deduplicated, any auto-block decision has been applied, and the alert has been prioritized and routed to the correct investigation queue. The event triggers triage and adjudication, where an investigator decides whether the alert carries enough signal to open a case. By modelling the hand-off as an explicit event, the bank can measure detection-to-investigation latency, enforce routing rules and severity prioritization, and maintain an audit trail linking the originating transaction to downstream case outcomes.
